context.project_description: Add token usage tracking to LLM provider calls so query costs can be monitored. This involves instrumenting chat methods in all LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama) to capture token counts from API responses and exposing this data through the SDK and API interfaces.
key_requirements: Instrument chat() and chat_stream() methods across all LLM provider implementations to capture token usage
Store token usage data in a context-aware manner for each request
Expose token usage information in Answer objects returned by ask() and query() methods
Include token usage in API response payloads when queries are processed
constraints: Do not modify provider method signatures to avoid breaking existing integrations
Leverage existing instrumentation system (InstrumentedProxy) rather than creating new hooks
Support different tokenization schemes across various LLM providers
Make token tracking configurable to prevent breaking changes for users who don't require it
context.existing_context: fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py, fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py, fitz_ai/llm/providers/anthropic.py, fitz_ai/llm/providers/cohere.py, fitz_ai/llm/providers/ollama.py, fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py, fitz_ai/sdk/fitz.py, fitz_ai/api/routes/query.py
stakeholders: developer requesting token usage tracking to monitor LLM query costs
system architect maintaining codebase modularity and avoiding coupling

in_scope: instrumenting chat and chat_stream methods in LLM providers to capture token usage
modifying ask/query methods in fitz.py to include token usage in returned Answer objects
extending API routes in query.py to expose token usage in response payloads
out_of_scope: modifying provider method signatures to add token tracking parameters
tracking streaming response tokens beyond basic integration points
adding opt-in configuration for token tracking (this is considered a default behavior)

existing_files: fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py — Provider protocols for LLM clients
fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py — OpenAI provider wrappers using the official SDK
fitz_ai/llm/providers/anthropic.py — Anthropic provider wrappers using the official SDK
fitz_ai/llm/providers/cohere.py — Cohere provider wrappers using the official SDK
fitz_ai/llm/providers/ollama.py — Ollama provider wrappers using direct HTTP calls
fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py — Instrumentation system for benchmarking plugin performance
needed_artifacts: fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py — Add token_usage field to RerankResult dataclass and update ChatProvider protocol to return token usage metadata
fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py — Extend InstrumentedProxy with token tracking capabilities or add new hook interface for token counting
fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py — Modify OpenAIChat.chat and OpenAIChat.chat_stream to capture and return token usage from API responses
fitz_ai/llm/providers/anthropic.py — Modify AnthropicChat.chat and AnthropicChat.chat_stream to capture token usage from API responses where available
fitz_ai/llm/providers/cohere.py — Modify CohereChat.chat and CohereChat.chat_stream to capture token usage from API responses if supported by provider
fitz_ai/llm/providers/ollama.py — Modify OllamaChat.chat and OllamaChat.chat_stream to capture token usage from HTTP response headers or body if available
fitz_ai/core/answer.py — Add tokens_used field to Answer dataclass for storing per-query token usage metadata

assumptions.assumption: LLM provider APIs consistently return token usage information in their responses
assumptions.impact: If untrue, token tracking will fail or produce inaccurate data for some providers; may require custom parsing logic per-provider

assumptions.assumption: The existing instrumentation system can be extended to capture and store token usage without significant architectural changes
assumptions.impact: If incorrect, would necessitate refactoring the instrumentation framework or using alternative mechanisms for tracking

assumptions.assumption: Token counting logic will not significantly impact performance of LLM calls due to minimal overhead from parsing responses
assumptions.impact: If untrue, could introduce latency in query processing that might be noticeable at scale

assumptions.assumption: Users of the system do not rely on current behavior where no token usage is tracked and thus won't experience breaking changes by adding this feature as opt-in/configurable
assumptions.impact: If users depend on specific response structures or behaviors, introducing token tracking may cause unexpected side effects unless explicitly opted in

assumptions.assumption: Token usage per model is consistent enough to allow centralized handling with minimal provider-specific adaptations
assumptions.impact: If untrue, would require heavy customization for each LLM provider (e.g., different tokenization schemes), increasing complexity and maintenance burden

assumptions.assumption: Streaming responses can be instrumented to capture intermediate token counts reliably
assumptions.impact: If incorrect, may miss tokens from partial chunks or fail to aggregate them correctly; would need separate handling for stream-based counting

approaches.name: Structured Response Types with Optional Usage Fields
approaches.description: Modify base protocols to return structured response types that include token usage metadata, updating all concrete implementations to parse and return this data from API responses.
pros: Provides clear, standardized interface for token tracking across all LLM providers
Enables accurate cost monitoring and optimization capabilities
Maintains backward compatibility through optional fields where possible
cons: Breaking change to existing provider interfaces requiring consumer updates
Significant refactoring effort across multiple provider implementations
Requires careful handling of varying API response formats from different LLM providers
best_for: Systems requiring precise token usage tracking and cost optimization
Production environments where LLM costs need monitoring

approaches.name: Instrumentation-Based Aggregation with Context Propagation
approaches.description: Enhance the existing instrumentation system to track token usage through method interception, using request-scoped context to accumulate totals across multiple provider calls within a single query execution.
pros: Leverages existing infrastructure without major interface changes
Supports cross-call aggregation naturally within query flows
Minimal disruption to current codebase architecture
cons: Requires new hook interfaces that don't currently exist in the benchmark system
Context propagation complexity when dealing with streaming responses and async operations
May not capture all token usage due to limitations in current instrumentation design
best_for: Systems wanting minimal interface changes while enabling aggregation
Environments where immediate implementation is preferred over long-term structural changes

architecture.recommended: Structured Response Types with Optional Usage Fields
architecture.reasoning: This approach directly addresses the fundamental architectural gaps identified in the analysis. The base protocols currently lack mechanisms to carry token metadata, and providers don't parse response usage fields. The recommended solution creates proper structured types that can propagate usage information through the entire call stack while maintaining extensibility with optional fields. The instrumentation-based alternative is insufficient because the BenchmarkHook protocol lacks capabilities for cross-call aggregation of token data. The structural approach also enables better testing and clearer interfaces that future-proof the system.

key_tradeoffs.structured_responses_vs_simplicity: Introducing structured response types with token metadata increases API complexity but provides essential cost tracking capabilities. Simple string returns are easier to implement but prevent usage aggregation and reporting.
key_tradeoffs.instrumentation_scope: The existing instrumentation system can intercept calls but lacks hooks for usage metrics - either extend it or build new mechanisms, creating a tradeoff between leveraging existing infrastructure vs. building specialized capability
key_tradeoffs.backward_compatibility_vs_enhanced_featu: Changing return types breaks existing consumers requiring migration paths; maintaining old interfaces risks missing out on valuable cost visibility features

technology_considerations: Python typing system for defining structured response types with optional usage fields
OpenAI SDK integration for parsing .usage.prompt_tokens and completion_tokens from responses
Ollama HTTP client behavior requiring JSON parsing of raw string content to extract usage data
Anthropic and Cohere SDKs that must be checked for similar usage field availability in responses
Context propagation mechanisms needed if multiple calls within a single query need aggregated token tracking
architecture.scope_statement: Refactor LLM provider protocols to include structured response types with token usage metadata, update all concrete implementations to parse API responses for usage fields, modify engine layers to aggregate totals, and extend API responses to return total query costs.

adrs.title: ADR: Add Structured Response Types for Token Usage Tracking
adrs.context: The base protocol interfaces in fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py are defined with simple return types that cannot carry token usage information, and all LLM provider implementations currently return raw string responses or embedding vectors without parsing the API's usage fields.
adrs.decision: Define structured response types (ChatResponse, EmbeddingResponse) in base.py that include token count and usage metadata, then modify concrete provider implementations to parse and return this data.
adrs.rationale: This approach enables comprehensive token tracking while maintaining backward compatibility through optional fields. The change is minimal yet impactful - it adds the necessary infrastructure for cost monitoring without disrupting existing functionality significantly.
consequences: Breaking changes to provider interfaces that will require updates in all consumer code
Providers must now parse API responses to extract usage data where available
Instrumentation system needs enhancement to aggregate token totals across multiple calls within a single query execution flow
alternatives_considered: Alternative: Extend existing return types with additional fields – rejected because it would require invasive changes to all provider implementations and break existing code patterns
Alternative: Add usage tracking via side-channel hooks in instrumentation system – rejected because current BenchmarkHook protocol lacks mechanisms for cross-call aggregation of token data

adrs.title: ADR: Modify Provider Implementations to Parse Token Usage from API Responses
adrs.context: All LLM provider implementations make API calls but do not extract token information from responses; OpenAI's SDK returns raw strings without accessing .usage.prompt_tokens, Ollama methods process JSON or string data with no token parsing.
adrs.decision: Update concrete provider classes (OpenAIChat, AnthropicChat, CohereChat, OllamaChat) to parse usage fields from API responses where available and return structured response types that include token counts.
adrs.rationale: This ensures accurate tracking of actual tokens consumed by each LLM call. It's necessary because providers are currently ignoring valuable usage metadata returned by their respective SDKs or APIs.
consequences: Each provider implementation must be updated individually to extract and format token data correctly
New dependencies may arise if API response structures vary significantly between providers (e.g., OpenAI vs Cohere vs Ollama)
Fallback handling needed for cases where usage fields are missing or malformed in responses
alternatives_considered: Alternative: Use wrapper classes that capture usage outside of provider logic – rejected because it's less maintainable and doesn't integrate cleanly with existing call stacks
Alternative: Store token data separately using context propagation – rejected because requires extensive changes to current architecture without clear benefit over direct integration

adrs.title: ADR: Extend API Response Format to Include Token Usage Information
adrs.context: The existing instrumentation system can intercept method calls but only receives raw return values, and there's no mechanism in place to aggregate token totals across multiple queries or calls during a single execution flow.
adrs.decision: Update QueryResponse model in fitz_ai/api/routes/query.py to include total token usage for each query response, which will be populated by the engine layer after aggregating individual provider call results.
adrs.rationale: This provides users with immediate visibility into costs associated with their queries while requiring minimal changes beyond schema modifications. It enables cost-aware applications and supports billing/monitoring features without complex infrastructure.
consequences: API clients will need to adapt to the new token_usage field in QueryResponse
Engine layer must be modified to accumulate usage data from multiple provider calls during answer generation
Frontend dashboards or tools consuming this API may require updates to display cost information
alternatives_considered: Alternative: Return token usage only at the application level instead of API endpoint – rejected because it reduces transparency and makes tracking harder for end users who want immediate insight into costs
Alternative: Keep usage data internal within engine layer without exposing through APIs – rejected because cost visibility is a primary requirement that needs to reach user-facing interfaces

components.name: BenchmarkHook

responsibilities: Provide methods to hook into method call start and end events for performance tracking
interfaces: on_call_start(layer: str, plugin_name: str, method: str, args: tuple, kwargs: dict) -> Any
on_call_end(context: Any, result: Any, error: Exception | None) -> None

components.name: CachingHook
components.purpose: Protocol defining hooks for caching LLM calls
responsibilities: Provide methods to hook into getting and caching results of method calls
interfaces: get_cached_result(layer: str, plugin_name: str, method: str, args: tuple, kwargs: dict) -> Any
cache_result(layer: str, plugin_name: str, method: str, args: tuple, kwargs: dict, result: Any) -> None

components.name: InstrumentedProxy
components.purpose: Wrapper that adds instrumentation to target objects
responsibilities: Wrap methods of target objects with instrumentation for tracking and logging
interfaces: __init__(target: Any, layer: str, plugin_name: str, methods_to_track: set[str] | None)

components.name: FitzKragEngine

responsibilities: Handle query processing, caching, and retrieval of information from indexed content
interfaces: _store_cloud_cache(query_text: str, addresses: list, answer: Answer) -> None
point(source: Path, collection: str | None) -> Any
_fast_index_code_files(manifest: Any, source_dir: Path, progress: Callable[[str], None] | None) -> None

components.name: fitz
components.purpose: SDK interface for interacting with the Fitz KRAG framework
responsibilities: Provide a user-friendly API to point data sources and ask questions about them
interfaces: __init__(collection: str, config_path: Optional[Union[str, Path]], auto_init: bool) -> None
ask(question: str, top_k: Optional[int], conversation_context: Optional['ConversationContext']) -> Answer

components.name: QueryRequest
components.purpose: Model representing a query request to the API endpoint
responsibilities: Contain all necessary data for processing a user's query

components.name: QueryResponse
components.purpose: Model representing the response from a query API endpoint
responsibilities: Wrap answer data and any metadata associated with it including token usage

components.name: ChatRequest
components.purpose: Model representing a chat request to the API endpoint
responsibilities: Contain all necessary data for processing a user's chat message

components.name: ChatResponse
components.purpose: Model representing the response from a chat API endpoint
responsibilities: Wrap chat messages and any metadata associated with it including token usage

integration_points: ExternalSystem — OpenAI API for chat, embedding, and vision capabilities
ExternalSystem — Anthropic API for chat and vision capabilities
ExternalSystem — Cohere API for chat, embedding, and rerank capabilities
ExternalSystem — Ollama API for chat, embedding, rerank, and vision capabilities

artifacts.content:
from __future__ import annotations

type ModelTier = str

from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Any, Iterator, Protocol


class RerankResult:
    """
    Result from reranking a document.
    """
    document: str
    score: float


class ChatProvider(Protocol):
    def chat(self, messages: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> str: ...

class StreamingChatProvider(Protocol):
    def chat_stream(self, messages: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> Iterator[str]: ...

class EmbeddingProvider(Protocol):
    def embed(self, text: str) -> list[float]: ...
    def embed_batch(self, texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]]: ...
    def dimensions(self) -> int: ...

class RerankProvider(Protocol):
    def rerank(self, query: str, documents: list[str], top_n: int | None) -> list[RerankResult]: ...

class VisionProvider(Protocol):
    def describe_image(self, image_base64: str, prompt: str | None) -> str: ...
artifacts.purpose: Base protocol interfaces for LLM clients that need to be updated to support token usage tracking
OpenAI provider wrappers that need modification to parse token usage from SDK responses
Ollama provider wrappers that need modification to parse token usage from HTTP responses
Instrumentation system that needs enhancement to support token usage aggregation

artifacts.content:
from __future__ import annotations

dataclass
from typing import Optional

from fitz_ai.core.answer_mode import AnswerMode
from fitz_ai.core.provenance import Provenance


class Answer:
    text: str
    sources: list[Provenance]
    mode: AnswerMode = AnswerMode.FULL
    token_usage: int = 0
    
    def __post_init__(self):
        if not isinstance(self.sources, list):
            raise ValueError("sources must be a list")
        # Ensure all sources are Provenance objects or convert them
        self.sources = [src if isinstance(src, Provenance) else Provenance(**src)
                        for src in self.sources]
artifacts.purpose: Answer class that needs to include token_usage field for reporting

components.purpose: Concrete implementation of RerankProvider for Ollama models
responsibilities: Re-rank documents using Ollama reranking models
interfaces: __init__(model: str | None, base_url: str | None) -> None
rerank(query: str, documents: list[str], top_n: int | None) -> list[RerankResult]

components.name: OllamaVision
components.purpose: Concrete implementation of VisionProvider for Ollama vision models
responsibilities: Describe images using Ollama vision models
interfaces: __init__(model: str | None, base_url: str | None) -> None
describe_image(image_base64: str, prompt: str | None) -> str

components.name: BenchmarkHook
components.purpose: Protocol defining hooks for benchmarking LLM calls
responsibilities: Provide methods to hook into method call start and end events for performance tracking
interfaces: on_call_start(layer: str, plugin_name: str, method: str, args: tuple, kwargs: dict) -> Any
on_call_end(context: Any, result: Any, error: Exception | None) -> None

components.name: CachingHook
components.purpose: Protocol defining hooks for caching LLM calls
responsibilities: Provide methods to hook into getting and caching results of method calls
interfaces: get_cached_result(layer: str, plugin_name: str, method: str, args: tuple, kwargs: dict) -> Any
cache_result(layer: str, plugin_name: str, method: str, args: tuple, kwargs: dict, result: Any) -> None

components.name: InstrumentedProxy
components.purpose: Wrapper that adds instrumentation to target objects
responsibilities: Wrap methods of target objects with instrumentation for tracking and logging
interfaces: __init__(target: Any, layer: str, plugin_name: str, methods_to_track: set[str] | None)

components.name: FitzKragEngine
components.purpose: Main engine for knowledge routing augmented generation
responsibilities: Handle query processing, caching, and retrieval of information from indexed content
interfaces: _store_cloud_cache(query_text: str, addresses: list, answer: Answer) -> None
point(source: Path, collection: str | None) -> Any
_fast_index_code_files(manifest: Any, source_dir: Path, progress: Callable[[str], None] | None) -> None

components.name: fitz
components.purpose: SDK interface for interacting with the Fitz KRAG framework
responsibilities: Provide a user-friendly API to point data sources and ask questions about them
interfaces: __init__(collection: str, config_path: Optional[Union[str, Path]], auto_init: bool) -> None
ask(question: str, top_k: Optional[int], conversation_context: Optional['ConversationContext']) -> Answer

components.name: QueryRequest
components.purpose: Model representing a query request to the API endpoint
responsibilities: Contain all necessary data for processing a user's query

components.name: QueryResponse
components.purpose: Model representing the response from a query API endpoint
responsibilities: Wrap answer data and any metadata associated with it including token usage

components.name: ChatRequest
components.purpose: Model representing a chat request to the API endpoint
responsibilities: Contain all necessary data for processing a user's chat message

components.name: ChatResponse
components.purpose: Model representing the response from a chat API endpoint
responsibilities: Wrap chat messages and any metadata associated with it including token usage

integration_points: ExternalSystem — OpenAI API for chat, embedding, and vision capabilities
ExternalSystem — Anthropic API for chat and vision capabilities
ExternalSystem — Cohere API for chat, embedding, and rerank capabilities
ExternalSystem — Ollama API for chat, embedding, rerank, and vision capabilities

artifacts.content:
from __future__ import annotations

type ModelTier = str

from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Any, Iterator, Protocol


class RerankResult:
    """
    Result from reranking a document.
    """
    document: str
    score: float


class ChatProvider(Protocol):
    def chat(self, messages: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> str: ...

class StreamingChatProvider(Protocol):
    def chat_stream(self, messages: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> Iterator[str]: ...

class EmbeddingProvider(Protocol):
    def embed(self, text: str) -> list[float]: ...
    def embed_batch(self, texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]]: ...
    def dimensions(self) -> int: ...

class RerankProvider(Protocol):
    def rerank(self, query: str, documents: list[str], top_n: int | None) -> list[RerankResult]: ...

class VisionProvider(Protocol):
    def describe_image(self, image_base64: str, prompt: str | None) -> str: ...
artifacts.purpose: Base protocol interfaces for LLM clients that need to be updated to support token usage tracking
OpenAI provider wrappers that need modification to parse token usage from SDK responses
Ollama provider wrappers that need modification to parse token usage from HTTP responses
Instrumentation system that needs enhancement to support token usage aggregation

artifacts.content:
from __future__ import annotations

dataclass
from typing import Optional

from fitz_ai.core.answer_mode import AnswerMode
from fitz_ai.core.provenance import Provenance


class Answer:
    text: str
    sources: list[Provenance]
    mode: AnswerMode = AnswerMode.FULL
    token_usage: int = 0
    
    def __post_init__(self):
        if not isinstance(self.sources, list):
            raise ValueError("sources must be a list")
        # Ensure all sources are Provenance objects or convert them
        self.sources = [src if isinstance(src, Provenance) else Provenance(**src)
                        for src in self.sources]
artifacts.purpose: Answer class that needs to include token_usage field for reporting

phases.name: Read Before Writing
phases.objective: Verify integration points by reading source
phases.verification_command: pytest fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_base.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_openai.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_anthropic.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_cohere.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_ollama.py

phases.name: Instrument Provider Wrappers
phases.objective: Wrap LLM providers with InstrumentedProxy to intercept method calls for token usage tracking
key_risks: Provider APIs may not return consistent token metadata formats
Ollama and other self-hosted models may not expose usage metrics directly

phases.name: Implement Token Usage Hook
phases.objective: Create a BenchmarkHook to extract and aggregate token usage from provider responses
deliverables: Logic to parse token metadata from different provider response formats
key_risks: Inconsistent API response structures across providers
Missing usage fields in some provider responses

phases.name: Register and Test Hook
phases.objective: Integrate the token usage hook into the system and validate it captures usage data correctly
deliverables: Hook registration in engine initialization flow
End-to-end test verifying token counting works across providers
key_risks: Timing issues with hook registration before provider usage
Performance overhead from instrumentation in production

risks.description: LLM providers vary widely in how they expose token usage metadata, with some (like Ollama) not providing it directly in API responses
risks.mitigation: Implement provider-specific logic to extract token counts from response objects and fall back to client-side estimation for unsupported APIs
risks.contingency: Log usage estimates with warning indicators when exact token data is unavailable, use input length heuristics as fallback for embedding providers
risks.verification: Assert that all wrapped provider instances have their usage captured in the registered hook's token_usage dict

risks.description: Instrumentation system assumes response objects contain usage metadata in consistent format across providers but OpenAI, Anthropic and Ollama differ
risks.mitigation: Create provider-specific parsing logic within the TokenUsageHook to handle different response structures for prompt_tokens/completion_tokens/total_tokens fields
risks.contingency: Fail gracefully by logging warnings when unexpected response format encountered, defaulting to zero token count in such cases
risks.verification: Assert that TokenUsageHook correctly parses usage from OpenAI-style responses and handles Ollama-style JSON with 'usage' key

risks.description: Adding token tracking may introduce overhead to LLM calls which could impact latency or throughput
risks.mitigation: Ensure instrumentation is optimized and only enabled in environments where usage metrics are required, use efficient wrapping techniques with minimal overhead
risks.contingency: Provide feature flag to disable token tracking when not needed for performance-critical deployments
risks.verification: Assert that benchmark timings show no significant increase in latency for wrapped provider calls compared to baseline

recommended_contingencies: Implement fallback client-side estimation using input length when exact token data is unavailable from LLM providers
Add feature flag to disable instrumentation overhead in performance-critical environments
Log warning messages with usage estimates for unsupported provider response formats

phases.name: Read Before Writing
phases.objective: Verify integration points by reading source
phases.verification_command: pytest fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_base.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_openai.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_anthropic.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_cohere.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_ollama.py
